title: elymus humidorum
intro: Elymus humidorum, commonly known as the Himalayan wheat grass, is a type of flowering plant in the grass family. This plant is believed to have various health benefits, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and anti-diabetic properties, which may help to reduce the risk of chronic diseases such as cancer and diabetes. The therapeutic actions of Himalayan wheat grass include its ability to promote relaxation and reduce stress levels due to its sedative and adaptogenic properties, making it a popular ingredient in herbal teas and supplements. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other plant-derived compounds that contribute to its medicinal properties.
side_effects: Elymus humidorum can have side effects if used improperly, such as skin irritation, allergic reactions, and respiratory problems. Ingesting the plant's leaves or seeds can lead to nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea. Prolonged exposure to the plant's pollen or oils can cause headaches, dizziness, and fatigue. Improper handling of the plant can also trigger asthma attacks or other breathing difficulties in sensitive individuals.
